01 — About

Who I am

I'm a first-year student at Fulbright University Vietnam, majoring in Applied Mathematics with a minor in Computer Science — on a full President's Scholarship.

My projects span statistical analysis, machine learning, AI product design, financial optimization, and safety technology. I'm drawn to problems where rigorous quantitative thinking meets real human needs.

Outside the classroom, I lead MindFulbright (a campus mental health club with 200+ participants per event), teach English to monks and nuns, and have organized community initiatives reaching 3,000+ people. I believe the best technologists are also deeply human.

04 — Work

All Projects

2024 — 2026
LotusHacks 202601
SafePath — AI Solo Travel Guardian

Real-time AI safety app that silently monitors your route and auto-alerts emergency contacts when you can't respond. Built in 36 hours at Vietnam's largest AI hackathon.

🌐 Demo ⌥ GitHub
React NativeFirebaseAI AgentTrackAsia
What it is
A proactive safety guardian for women traveling alone — instead of waiting for you to call for help, SafePath detects when something is wrong and escalates automatically. Built by a team of four girls, two of whom have personally experienced harassment while traveling alone.
What I did
  • Defined the problem from lived experience — harassment on everyday routes is routine, not rare
  • Designed the 4-layer system: Tracking → Anomaly Detection → AI Reasoning → Escalation
  • Implemented anomaly detection: deviation >50m, stationary 5min, trip exceeding ETA
  • Integrated OpenRouter AI layer to reduce false positives by understanding context
  • Built the silent intervention flow: vibration → "Are you safe?" → countdown → auto-alert
What I learned
The hardest part wasn't the code — it was turning emotional experience into system logic. Safety must be proactive, not reactive. The best products are built by people who feel the problem in their own life.
Intro to Statistics02
Doctor Stress Level Analysis

Statistical study on 100+ doctors examining how sleep, gender, and career stage affect stress levels.

Hypothesis TestingANOVAPearson rR
What it is
A statistical study on 100+ doctors examining how sleep duration, gender, and career stage affect stress — final project for Intro to Statistics.
What I did
  • Built visualizations (histograms, box plots, scatter plots)
  • Ran one-sample t-test comparing doctors' sleep against the recommended 7 hours
  • Conducted two-sample hypothesis testing: male vs. female stress
  • Applied one-way ANOVA + Fisher's LCD post-hoc across 3 age groups
  • Computed Pearson correlation (r = −0.566) between sleep and stress
What I learned
Sleep explains 32% of stress variance — significant but not the whole story. Choosing the right test matters as much as running it.
Linear Algebra03
Book Recommendation via Cosine Similarity

Item-item collaborative filtering using matrix operations — deployed as a live web app for personalized book recommendations.

Collaborative FilteringMatrix MathPythonWeb App
What it is
A working book recommendation engine using item-item collaborative filtering — deployed as a live website where users enter a title and receive personalized suggestions.
What I did
  • Built a user-item rating matrix from Kaggle data with NaN imputation
  • Applied mean-centering normalization to remove rating bias
  • Computed cosine similarity between book vectors to find nearest neighbors
  • Filtered to users with 10+ ratings and books with 4+ ratings
  • Deployed the full pipeline as an interactive web application
What I learned
Cosine similarity is literally just the dot product of normalized vectors — seeing a math concept from class power a real product was a turning point for me.
Probability04
Stock Price Jump-Diffusion Model

Probabilistic model separating normal stock movements from sudden jumps — with 5,000 Monte Carlo simulations and an interactive Streamlit app.

Monte CarloStochastic ModelPythonStreamlit
What it is
A discrete-time Jump-Diffusion model separating "normal" stock drift from sudden shocks — using 5,000 Monte Carlo simulations to forecast price distributions and risk probabilities.
What I did
  • Computed daily log-returns and detected jump days using 3-sigma threshold
  • Classified jumps as "crisis" or "regular" via Modified Z-scores and MAD
  • Estimated diffusion parameters (μ, σ²) from non-jump returns
  • Built Monte Carlo engine generating 5,000 price paths with confidence intervals
  • Deployed as interactive Streamlit app for any stock ticker
What I learned
Real markets aren't Gaussian. Modeling tail events — not just the average — is what separates useful risk models from dangerously naive ones.
Quantitative Finance05
Sin Stock Portfolio Optimization

Quantified the "cost of virtue" in ESG investing using quadratic programming and Modern Portfolio Theory — backtested over 14 rolling quarters.

MPTSLSQPBacktestingscipy
What it is
A quant finance study asking: how much extra risk do ethical investors actually bear by excluding tobacco, alcohol, and gambling stocks? We called it "the cost of virtue."
What I did
  • Formulated portfolio optimization as Quadratic Programming (minimize wᵀΣw)
  • Implemented SLSQP solver with 4 constraints: budget, return target, no short-selling, sin cap
  • Ran scenario analysis across sin caps of 0%, 25%, 50%, 100%
  • Built rolling-window backtesting: 3-year train, 1-quarter test, 14 periods total
  • Constructed efficient frontiers for Sin-only, Non-Sin, and Full portfolios
What I learned
The optimal sin allocation was ~23% — beyond that, variance stops decreasing. Ethics has a measurable price (~8.6% more risk at 0% sin cap), but it's not as catastrophic as some assume.
Design & Systems Thinking06
Smart Medicine Box

Human-centered design project building a smart medication reminder for elderly users — from empathy maps to working hardware prototype.

▶ Watch Demo
UX ResearchPrototypingHardwareSystems Thinking
What it is
A human-centered design project solving medication non-adherence for elderly users — from user research to a physical-digital prototype with timer display and compartment system.
What I did
  • Conducted user research and built empathy maps to identify pain points
  • Defined problem space using systems thinking frameworks
  • Prototyped a smart box with reminder alerts and compartment tracking
  • Pitched with full design rationale to a panel of judges
What I learned
The hardest part of design isn't the prototype — it's resisting the urge to jump to solutions before truly understanding the problem. Empathy first, technology second.
Calculus I07
Taylor Series: From Zeno to Rocket Science

Mathematical essay proving Taylor Series from first principles — through Euler's Identity, convergence analysis, and Falcon 9 stabilization.

Mathematical ProofAnalysisLaTeX
What it is
A research essay tracing Taylor Series from Zeno's paradoxes and the Kerala School of Mathematics to modern engineering — with original proofs and real-world applications from calculators to aerospace.
What I did
  • Derived Taylor Series from scratch using method of matching derivatives
  • Proved Lagrange Error Bound via Rolle's Theorem
  • Applied Ratio Test to determine radius of convergence for ln(1+x)
  • Proved Euler's Identity (e^iπ + 1 = 0) from series expansions
  • Showed how sin(θ) ≈ θ enables Falcon 9 stabilization at 1000Hz
What I learned
Madhava in 14th-century India derived trigonometric series 200 years before European mathematicians — yet history barely mentions him. Math is not discovered in a straight line, and whose work gets credited matters.
GDGOC Hackathon 202508
Thân Thanh — AI Alzheimer Companion

AI audiobook platform cloning a family member's voice to support Alzheimer's patients — Top 12 out of 100+ teams across Southern Vietnam.

Voice CloningHealthcare AIFlutterResponsible AI
What it is
An AI platform bridging physical Alzheimer's care and emotional support — cloning a loved one's voice to read audiobooks, improving memory recall and reducing patient anxiety.
What I did
  • Researched non-pharmacological Alzheimer's interventions
  • Designed the full product: voice pipeline (Whisper → FastSpeech 2 → HiFi-GAN)
  • Built responsible AI framework: consent, access controls, psychological risk mitigation
  • Developed prototype website demonstrating core user journey
  • Pitched to judges at GDGOC — Top 12 from 100+ teams
What I learned
Responsible AI isn't a checklist — it's a design constraint from day one. Voice cloning of deceased relatives can cause real psychological harm if misused. Ethics before code.
